headline: Scoop: JC Vance raises $4.2 million in Silicon Valley
summary: Vice President JD Vance headlined an RNC fundraiser on Thursday night in Palo Alto, California, at the residence of influential investor and All-In Podcast host Chamath Palihapitiya , raking in $4.2 million for the Republican Party, a source familiar with the dinner tells Axios.

headline: The AI Selloff Is Getting Brutal: 10 Tech Giants Already Deep in Bear Market Territory
summary: The AI boom promised to create a new generation of market winners. Instead, it has also exposed just how quickly expectations can outrun reality. After years of paying premium valuations for anything tied to artificial intelligence, investors are now demanding stronger earnings, disciplined spending, and proof that massive AI investments will generate real returns. The ... The AI Selloff Is Getting Brutal: 10 Tech Giants Already Deep in Bear Market Territory

headline: Coinbase (COIN) Secures MiCA License And Opens Luxembourg Hub For 27 EU States
summary: Coinbase Global (NasdaqGS:COIN) has set up a new EU crypto hub in Luxembourg after receiving a MiCA license. The license grants Coinbase access to offer regulated services across all 27 EU member states. The move comes as other global crypto platforms reassess their EU plans in response to the MiCA framework. For investors tracking Coinbase Global, the timing of this regulatory milestone comes after a sharp pullback in the stock. NasdaqGS:COIN closed at $142.52, with the share price down...

headline: Is MSTR Stock's 40% Bitcoin Discount A Generational Buy Or An Optical Illusion?
summary: Strategy’s (MSTR) collapse to $85, marking a severe 76% slide over the past year, reflects a sharp narrative repricing. The market’s premier corporate Bitcoin proxy faced institutional derisking after management demonstrated a willingness to utilize core treasury assets for liquidity, shifting Wall Street perception from an untouchable cold-storage vault into an actively managed corporate balance sheet.

content.title: Is MSTR Stock's 40% Bitcoin Discount A Generational Buy Or An Optical Illusion?

headline: Does the Clarity Act have a human trafficking blind spot?
summary: CoinDesk's The Policy Protocol hosts Rebecca Rettig and Renato Mariotti open with two hot topics: Coinbase's SEC-registered AI agentic trading launch and tribal gaming operators' letter to Senators Thune and Schumer demanding that the CLARITY Act include a prohibition on prediction markets. Then Katie Boller Gosewisch, Executive Director of the Alliance to End Human Trafficking, joins to challenge CLARITY's Section 604, arguing that shielding DeFi developers from money-transmitter liability creates a "duty of care" gap that traffickers can exploit. Plus, Rebecca and Renato close by naming Meta as their Person of the Week for its reported move into points-based prediction markets.

content.title: Does the Clarity Act have a human trafficking blind spot?
content.description: CoinDesk's The Policy Protocol hosts Rebecca Rettig and Renato Mariotti open with two hot topics: Coinbase's SEC-registered AI agentic trading launch and tribal gaming operators' letter to Senators Thune and Schumer demanding that the CLARITY Act include a prohibition on prediction markets. Then Katie Boller Gosewisch, Executive Director of the Alliance to End Human Trafficking, joins to challenge CLARITY's Section 604, arguing that shielding DeFi developers from money-transmitter liability creates a "duty of care" gap that traffickers can exploit. Plus, Rebecca and Renato close by naming Meta as their Person of the Week for its reported move into points-based prediction markets.
content.summary: CoinDesk's The Policy Protocol hosts Rebecca Rettig and Renato Mariotti open with two hot topics: Coinbase's SEC-registered AI agentic trading launch and tribal gaming operators' letter to Senators Thune and Schumer demanding that the CLARITY Act include a prohibition on prediction markets. Then Katie Boller Gosewisch, Executive Director of the Alliance to End Human Trafficking, joins to challenge CLARITY's Section 604, arguing that shielding DeFi developers from money-transmitter liability creates a "duty of care" gap that traffickers can exploit. Plus, Rebecca and Renato close by naming Meta as their Person of the Week for its reported move into points-based prediction markets.
